have h₁ : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    infer_instance
  -- We need to show that the pushforward measure is a probability measure.
  -- This follows because the preimage of the whole space is the whole space.
  have h₂ : (wignerMatrixMeasure n μ ν) (Set.univ : Set (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ)) = 1 := by
    -- The pushforward of a probability measure through a measurable function is a probability measure.
    calc
      (wignerMatrixMeasure n μ ν) (Set.univ : Set (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ)) =
          (Measure.map (wignerMatrixMap n) (WignerMeasure μ ν n)) (Set.univ : Set (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ)) := rfl
      _ = (WignerMeasure μ ν n) ((wignerMatrixMap n)⁻¹' (Set.univ : Set (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ))) := by
        rw [Measure.map_apply]
      _ = (WignerMeasure μ ν n) (Set.univ : Set (WignerSpace n)) := by
        congr 1
        ext x
        simp
        <;>
        (try simp_all [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction])
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      _ = 1 := by
        -- Since WignerMeasure μ ν n is a probability measure, it assigns 1 to the universal set.
        have h₃ : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := h₁
        exact h₃.univ
  exact isProbabilityMeasure_iff.mpr h₂

have h₁ : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    rw [WignerMeasure]

    -- Since each coordinate is either `μ` or `ν`, and both are probability measures,
    -- the product is also a probability measure.
    apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.isProbabilityMeasure
    intro i
    by_cases h : i.val.1 = i.val.2
    · -- If the indices are equal, the measure is `ν`, which is a probability measure.
      simp [h, wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> exact inferInstance
    · -- If the indices are not equal, the measure is `μ`, which is a probability measure.
      simp [h, wignerMeasureDist]
      <;> exact inferInstance
  -- Since `wignerMatrixMap n` is measurable and `WignerMeasure μ ν n` is a probability measure,
  -- the pushforward is also a probability measure.
  have h₂ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    apply wignerMatrixMapMeasurable
  -- Use the fact that the pushforward of a probability measure under a measurable map is a probability measure.
  have h₃ : IsProbabilityMeasure (Measure.map (wignerMatrixMap n) (WignerMeasure μ ν n)) := by
    apply h₁.map_measurable h₂
  -- The `wignerMatrixMeasure` is defined as the pushforward of `WignerMeasure μ ν n` under `wignerMatrixMap n`.
  simpa [wignerMatrixMeasure] using h₃
